reviews.review_description: Overall game looks good, but I can see the issues as reported by other people. Shots are not accurate. Plane controls are finnicky. I don't want to give up the game but if there are no updates within the refund window, I probably will ask for the refund.

Hey guys - Did you ever test this game? Shots don‘t go out straight. They stay were they were fired!! … I hope for an update on Meta Quest 3 … Currently not playable! … Please bring an update quick ! …

developer_response.body:
Hi riddler75 — thanks for the heads‑up. The issue with shots not moving straight was a bug and it’s already been fixed in the latest updates (including on Meta Quest 3). Please give it another try and, if it’s working for you now, we’d really appreciate it if you could update your review.

If the problem persists on your device, please email us at support@ivanovichgames.com
 with your headset model and a short video so we can investigate. Thanks for helping us improve!

reviews.review_description:
So giving the game a second-chance and re-buying it, I have to say I'm enjoying it now. Just a really great arcade title to have, and I love any game that has satisfying progression in the form of great upgrades giving me more reason to keep playing. 
I will say though that I still find the Immersive Mode sections the weakest so far, minus the stationary shooting one that works fine. The controls for these sections is just so slow and cumbersome, making it hard to do what you need to do. They don't feel impossible/ bugged nor are crashing my game this time around though (not sure if the game has had a few updates since my last play 6+ months ago or if I was just in a bad place before, but happy no matter the case. 😅).

So I'm glad to have this in my collection again and definitely recommend it to any arcade fans and/or classic bullethell jet fighter games.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your detailed feedback. The issue you mentioned with the jet’s guns was indeed a bug, but we want to let you know that it has already been fixed in the latest updates. The weapons now work correctly in all modes.

We’d really appreciate it if you could revisit the game and, if everything works fine on your end, consider updating your review. Reviews are very important for small studios like ours, and your updated experience would help other players see the real state of the game today.

Thanks again for giving our game another chance and for being part of the community!

reviews.review_description: This game is very fun until you go to the immersion zone. It’s so incomplete. The controls and shooting is so bad. Doesn’t make sense. I don’t know why they even kept it in the game with it being that horrible. They could’ve just kept the immersion part out of the game completely. If there’s not an update to fix this, the game is not worth getting. ￼

developer_response.body:
Hi marcus151 — thanks for sharing your thoughts. The immersion zones are actually a favorite part for many players, but we understand they can feel strange at first. It usually takes a few tries to get used to the mechanics.

That said, by the time of 1976 we weren’t in a position to rebuild those sections completely. Based on feedback like yours, in 2076: Midway Multiverse we separated the immersion zones so players can enjoy them as standalone stages. Have you tried 2076 yet? There’s a free demo available and we’d love to know what you think!

reviews.review_description: Waited on this game for awhile. I just knew I wanted it as an old schooler. I thought for sure I would love it. Bam, it came up for sale $5.99. Gonna get a refund! They just try to do too much. Plus, I can't stand shooting games where it seems like your bullets don't do anything. At one point, I ran over some time warp thing..(time slows down) But still I threw everything I had into one little enemy & it wasn't enough!!?? They just flew on off the screen, never to be seen again.

developer_response.body:
Hi Deltablow — thanks for giving our game a try and for sharing your impressions. The issue with bullets not behaving correctly was a bug that we’ve already fixed in the latest updates, so enemies should now react as expected.

We’d really appreciate it if you could give it another shot and, if everything feels better, consider updating your review. And if you still notice problems, please reach out to us at support@ivanovichgames.com
 so we can investigate further. Your feedback truly helps us improve!

reviews.review_description: I was very surprised just how fun this was. Hitting perspective changing power ups turned a good 1941 style game into a great 1941/After burner mash up game. Would recommend to anyone who also likes arcade shooters and flight combat games.

Just bought the game, and it seems like the bullets won’t shoot out of the front of the plane. They kind of just spawn wherever your plane happens to be and then stay on that location until something hits it.

developer_response.body:
Hi MakerMike — thanks for reporting this. The bug you describe with the bullets was already identified and fixed in the latest updates, so the weapons should now work correctly.

Please give it another try and, if everything looks fine now, we’d really appreciate it if you could update your review. If the issue still happens on your device, please email us at support@ivanovichgames.com
 so we can investigate further. Your feedback is a big help!

reviews.review_description: Oh my... I remember dumping multiple quarters at the arcade playing games like this as kid. I've replayed them on home video games consoles as well. What this developer has done here is awesome! First, it takes the game and throws it on a huge screen and adds 3-D visuals which has never been done before. Second, it takes the game a step further and immerses the player in the cockpit of the plane in special access areas within the level you're playing. It's a very different experience than I remember as a kid and I am all for it. Gen X gamers will appreciate this experience most definitely.

Fun to play. BUT the controls in the IZ   Immersion zone are too sensitive and jerky ..  it’s almost impossible to hit planes .. fix this in an update and you’ll get 4 stars !! 
I love the game and can play for hours but the IZ zone controls are too janky ..  I would pay for an update that improves controls. And more 3D time in the IZ .

This really feels like they made a nice and very decent 1942 game, an overhead shooter with all the bells and whistles, then decided that they "need some 3D VR" and cobbled together the "Immersion Zone" part of the game as an afterthought in a single day without any beta testing whatsoever.

The controls and handling of the Immersion Zone are so incredibly bad, those parts of the game are virtually unplayable, regardless of what control system you choose.  Didn't they even test this or don't they just care? The difference in quality between the two game parts is quite jarring. The standard 1942 game play is quite nice, but the IZ is an atrocious mess in every respect, with the controls and handling being the worst. Good luck trying to shoot any plane down, apart from random lucky shots.

I could forgive the game if you could just turn the IZ off or just ignore those parts, but you still need to "collect stars" from your missions for your progress, which include the IZ, so you are stuck with having to play the IZ parts too. That immediately pulls the rating down to 2 stars, instead of 4 stars.

Either fix the IZ or give the player the option to turn it off without hampering their level progression! Not sure if I can trust Ivanovich Games with any future purchases, having experienced this obviously tacked on, shoddy gameplay.

This game is incredible, and definitely one of my favorite games on the Quest.  The first night I got it, I played for hours.  I’ve already made it up to the final stage (well, the final 2 I should say), and I’m getting my butt best big time!  I’m leveling up my plane like crazy so that way I can beat it.  If you like shmups and liked 1979, you will love this VR version.  This is DEFINITELY a must buy, and at only $15, how could you go wrong?  There are games twice this in price that aren’t as good!

reviews.review_title: Took me back to the arcade
reviews.review_description: I’ve only played an hour of this game so far, but I’ve very much enjoyed it. Feels like you’re up close and personal with the old arcade machine. The immersion side levels are fun, I need to play them seated because VR sickness. I want to tweak my controls a bit and see how the options other than fake VR flight stick feel.

This game is a steal for $15! So much fun, and the immersive zones look beautiful! My only issue is the controls in the immersive zones are a little wonky but I’m sure in time I’ll figure them out. Highly recommend this game!

This game rocks!

It's increïble how they manage to mantain the frantic essence of the original game and adding some very fun immersion modes but respectful with the original idea of the game.
There are little things to polish but it smells 5 star and worthy of being in the oficial app because it's much better than several games that run out there.
Congrats to the programmers!

Quite fun, immersive elements are pretty cool and I love the style.  A bit rough around the edges here and there, but altogether a fun time. Note that the immersive elements do not give you full control of the airplane - you are still going forward, which can be a bit off putting at first.
Excellent game cant wait for the new one 👍🏻

reviews.review_title: I played this as a coin-op
reviews.review_description:
I played this game as a coin-op game back in the '80s under a different name.  It was strange and difficult at first but the more I played it and learned the tricks, the more I enjoyed the game.  

You can go from playing it as a coin-op, looking at the screen to actually being inside the game and playing it in the first person.  

Like most VR games, more in-depth "how to play" instructions are needed.

There is a demo for a taste but this is a fun top down bullet hell arcade shooter. Then you switch into immersion zones, which have you flying the plane, like your the pilot in first person or third person or in a ball turret; you never know! It's a ton of fun, all the while you earn coins to upgrade your plane... Which is a time traveling plane using 1976 tech to go back in time and kill Nazi's. What more could you want?

This game takes me back to being 10yrs old at the mall arcade pumping quarters into the original game. This has all of the goodness from the original made up to date with the bonus of virtual reality immersion in immersion zones!

Also try the Operation Warcade demo and get your hands around an Uzi and let it rip! Another classic made better!!

To the developers,

What’s next? How about Commando remake? Or a single or co-op game with Ikari Warriors remake? There are so many awesome games you can remake.
Count me in on getting them all!
I thought it was a first person shooting game

developer_response.body: The game includes some sections in the first person, but, as seen in the trailer, it is a tribute to 1943 with a classic shoot-em-up view, and so that no one is left with doubts, we offer a free demo.
